channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti on both sides of rail bridge, 24th June
item.description:
Tags need to be removed asap
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crofton Lane, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Stop line and pedestrian studs faded, 24th June
item.description:
Stop line faded on - requires repainting. Pedestrian stud faded - requires repainting.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Eastway, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Littered street, 24th June
item.description:
Lots of litter on Combermere road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Combermere Road, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti sprayed on Lambeth charity clothes container at St John's Crescent SW9 directly by Max roach park., 24th June
item.description:
I like to report graffiti sprayed on a lambeth charity box container at the beginning of St John's Crescent SW9 Brixton by Max roach park. The colour of the containers is terracotta. The graffiti has been there for over a month. Please can it be removed.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St John's Crescent, Brixton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter from overflowing bins, 24th June
item.description:
Overflowing bin from 19 Combermere rd littering street. Ownerhad had notices sent by Lambeth before due to poor waste management from thiis holiday let

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 49, Combermere Road, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish bins full, 23rd June
item.description:
The bins in the area are all full and due to recent heat are being to smell foul. There is also evidence that fly tipping has begun where bins cannot fit any more
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Georges Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Silver Grey Ford Van EU66 WVJ, 23rd June
item.description:
Silver Grey Ford Van reg no EU66 WVJ - Untaxed (expired Mar 26) has been abandoned for over 5 months. Thisbis big van at the junction of Gipsy Road /Normanhurst Avenue and acts as an obstancle for vehicles trying to enter this junction via Swanley / Wrotham Road. It creates a blind spot and obstructs view far out.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Normanhurst Avenue, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street lights, 23rd June
item.description:
Street light not working for a week now
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Churchdown, Downham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown Vegetation, 23rd June
item.description:
The vegetation has broken through the pavement tarmac and paving slabs, causing them to crack and lift. In several sections, the weeds are now overgrown, dense, and sprawling across the walking path. Which is trip hazard

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dunoon Road, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked drains., 23rd June
item.description:
Three blocked gutter drains. We spent 4 hours washing the overflow of crap and mud out of our front garden this morning. We now can't open our windows!!!! due to the stench of the drain overflow. A high pressure washer truck is needed urgently. Our house is overheating!!!! Can't open the windows!!!! It keeps happening, time and time again. Sort it... NOW!!!!!!!!!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Warwick Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Community notice board still a mess, 23rd June
item.description:
The poor state of the community noticeboard was reported recently. An operative attended on Monday and left having made very little improvement. They had scraped the notices off the glass and left all the residue on the pavement around. Two information sheets had been haphazardly pinned up inside the noticeboard. There is no indication of who is responsible for putting up notices or where community members can apply to have their public interest notices put up. This was a job badly done, with little regard for the concerns expressed in the complaint. The noticeboard still needs an effective cleanup and more information on contacting key holders.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 83, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken bottles, 23rd June
item.description:
Broken bottles in the walk through from Lucy Way to rail sidings rd. Broken they have been for three days and not been cleared.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lucey Way, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 16 Melbourne Grove-bin needs to be emptied, 23rd June
item.description:
Bin needs to be emptied.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 16, Melbourne Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bags and rubbish, 23rd June
item.description:
Junction of Rail Sidings Rd and Lucey way service rd. Rubbish has accumulated over the last couple of weeks. Attracting rats and foxes. This is a known dumping spot so why is it not regularly patrolled and cleared. Your operators cleared a pile just 75 yards away the other day yet left this here when it would have been clearly visible to them. Why ??

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 9, Rail Sidings Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grove Vale-next to phone shop- bin needs to be emptied, 23rd June
item.description:
Bins needs to be emptied.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 26, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Bin needs emptying.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London

item.title: Slab underneath bin broken, bin been moved and is wonky, 23rd June
item.description:
Bin in park by football goals broken

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brook Way, St Paul's Cray, Greater London

item.title: Fly tipping, dumped bags, 23rd June
item.description:
Over a week ago someone has fly tipped/ dumped these bags at the end of Queenscroft Road. Can they please be removed.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queenscroft Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grove Vale-outside Marks and Spencer litter bin-overflowing with waste, 23rd June
item.description:
Bin needs to be emptied.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 18-22, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London

item.title: Parking permit sign uprooted, 23rd June
item.description:
A parking permit sign has come out of the ground.
